label: Mountain
comfortReason: High altitude, thin air, hard ground. Exposed rock offers no insulation. Wind cuts through everything.
scoutGuidance: Survival check. Poor: 2-7 | Average: 8-12 | Good: 13+. Ranger or druid has advantage. Nature proficiency helps identify stable ground.

nat1: A flat ledge tucked behind a ridge. Sheltered from the wind, solid ground.
A shallow cave under an overhang. Dry, defensible, out of sight from below.
A plateau with a natural stone windbreak. Perfect.
poor: A rocky shelf. Exposed, but flat.
A gulley between two ridges. Some wind cover.
average: A dry ledge with good sight lines.
A flat area behind a boulder field. Decent shelter.
A natural alcove in the cliff face.
good: A concealed shelf behind a rock spur. Invisible from below.
A dry cave mouth with clear approaches in both directions.
A ridge saddle with natural wind barriers.
nat20: An abandoned shepherd's shelter, stone walls still standing.
A hidden cave system, dry and warm from geothermal vents.
A clifftop eyrie with 360-degree views and a natural windbreak.
